question: Wi-Fi Protected Access 2 (WPA2)
canonical_answer:
- Implements the full IEEE 802.11i standard
- Mandatory to be Wi-Fi certified
- National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) FIPS 140-2 compliant
- Uses AES encryption
Two versions:
WPA2-Enterprise
WPA2-Personal

appears_in.title: Comptia A+ Free Study Guide
appears_in.answer: An improved version of WPA that does not support older network cards and offers both secure authentication and data encryption. It uses EAP for a variety of authentication methods—most often EAP-PSK.

appears_in.title: Comptia A+ Material

appears_in.title: Comptia Exam Objectives Security+
appears_in.answer: Site Surveys, Heat Maps, Wifi Analyzers, Channel Overlay, WAP, Controller and access point security

appears_in.title: Comptia Fundamentals Study Guide
appears_in.answer:
The strongest wireless encryption method currently
available for 802.11 networks.
